email_reminders.info_desc: When enabled, teachers who did not add a Class Diary entry for their scheduled classes on the previous day will receive an email reminder. The system checks the timetable to identify which teachers had classes and which ones are missing diary entries.
email_reminders.card_diary_subtitle: Enable or disable automatic email reminders for teachers
email_reminders.desc_enable: Send daily reminders to teachers with missing diary entries
email_reminders.hint_time: Reminders will be sent at this time (server timezone: Asia/Karachi)
email_reminders.card_days_subtitle: Select which days of the week to send reminders
email_reminders.hint_days: Reminders check for missing entries from the previous day's classes
email_reminders.card_test_subtitle: Send a test reminder email to verify your email configuration is working
email_reminders.msg_no_days: Please select at least one day for reminders

email_smtp.card_from_subtitle: Name and email shown to recipients
email_smtp.card_test_subtitle: Verify the connection by sending a test email. Uses the settings currently shown in the form (saves are not required first).

semester_rollover.title: Semester Rollover
semester_rollover.subtitle: Promote, retain, or drop students at the end of Semester 1 — within the same academic year.
semester_rollover.how_it_works_desc: Student outcomes are determined by their Rolling / Retention Option (rollover status set on each student's enrollment):
semester_rollover.item_pending_promoted: Pending / Promoted — moved to next grade (or graduated if no next grade)
semester_rollover.item_retained: Retained — stays in same grade, no change
semester_rollover.item_dropped: Dropped / Graduated / Transferred — enrollment closed at the semester end date
semester_rollover.how_it_works_footer: Set each student's status in Students → Enrollment before running this rollover.
semester_rollover.no_marking_periods_desc: No SEM-type marking periods found. You can still set a date manually.
semester_rollover.end_date_desc: Dropped / graduated students will have their enrollment closed on this date.
semester_rollover.preview_desc: What will happen to each group when rollover executes.
semester_rollover.active_students: {count, plural, =0 {No active students} one {# active student} other {# active students}}
semester_rollover.no_enrollments: No active enrollments found for this year{campusId, select, true { / campus} other {}}.
semester_rollover.action_pending: promoted to next grade (or graduated if no next grade)
semester_rollover.action_promoted: promoted to next grade (or graduated if no next grade)
semester_rollover.action_retained: no change — stays in same grade
semester_rollover.action_dropped: enrollment closed at semester end date
semester_rollover.action_graduated: enrollment closed at semester end date
semester_rollover.action_transferred: enrollment closed at semester end date
semester_rollover.confirm_question: Are you sure you want to roll students to Semester 2?
semester_rollover.warning_item_1: Promoted students will have their grade level updated immediately.
semester_rollover.warning_item_2: Dropped/Graduated/Transferred students will have enrollment closed.
semester_rollover.warning_item_3: Make sure to save report cards for Semester 1 before proceeding.
semester_rollover.warning_item_4: This action cannot be automatically undone.

overview.title: Academics
overview.subtitle: Manage your school's academic structure: grades, sections, and subjects
overview.grades_desc: Manage grade levels for your school. Grades are the foundation for sections and subjects.
overview.sections_desc: Create and manage classroom sections within each grade level.
overview.subjects_desc: Define curriculum subjects specific to each grade level.
overview.step1_desc: Everything starts here. Create grade levels like "Grade 10", "Matric", or "O-Levels". Each grade has an order index (for sorting) and a base monthly fee.
overview.step2_desc: Divide each grade into sections like "Section A", "Blue", or "Girls Wing". Set capacity limits to control enrollment.
overview.step3_desc: Define subjects specific to each grade. "Math for Grade 10" is different from "Math for Grade 1". This ensures teachers are only assigned relevant subjects.
overview.rule: You cannot create a Section without a Grade. You cannot assign a Subject without a Grade. Grade Level is the parent of everything.
